mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the manufacturing sector that boosts aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals a reduction in the supply of new homes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment signals an expansion in the labor market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable income signals an expansion in consumer spending that boosts aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real disposable income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a contraction in the labor market that boosts consumer savings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in the overall economy that boosts aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the consumer price index signals a reduction in inflation expectations that boosts real interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining CPI are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
